reasoning:
Based on the FOMC's actions and meeting schedules in 2025, there have been exactly two 25-basis-point rate cuts so far this year: one in September 2025 (lowering the target range to 4.00%-4.25%) and one in October 2025 (lowering the range to 3.75%-4.00%). A September 18, 2025 article explicitly noted the September cut was "the first rate cut since December 2024," confirming no cuts occurred between January and August. 

To reach exactly 4 rate cuts for 2025, the Fed would need to make two more 25-basis-point cuts (or a single 50-bps cut) at the sole remaining scheduled meeting for the year, which takes place on December 9-10. Current CME FedWatch data up to November 5, 2025, shows that the odds of even a standard 25-bps cut in December have fallen to 63.8%, as expectations have cooled due to economic data and the recent election/shutdown-related risks. Because the market is mostly pricing in either a 25-bps cut or no cut at all, the probability of an aggressive 50-bps cut in December (which is the only way to mathematically achieve exactly 4 cuts in 2025) is exceptionally low. Therefore, the chance of this market resolving 'Yes' is very small.
